unit_04 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_04

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 387 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 SE
Asserting a need to preserve the ______ that became the hallmark of her predecessor’s tenure, the new director of federal monetary policy refused to subscribe to rigid or mechanistic rules in policy making.Asserting a need to preserve the ______ that became the hallmark of her predecessor’s tenure, the new director of federal monetary policy refused to subscribe to rigid or mechanistic rules in policy making.
答案：B, F　adaptability; flexibility
hallmarksubscriberigid
hallmark: standard unit headword | subscribe: standard unit headword | rigid: standard unit headword
#2 TC DOUBLE
Recent scholarship has questioned the (i)______ of tropical forests around the world. Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)______ as well.Recent scholarship has questioned the (i)______ of tropical forests around the world. Archeologists have shown, for example, that the largest contiguous tract of what was thought to be virgin rain forest in the southern Amazon had been transformed into a cultural parkland before European contact, and many of the forest islands in West Africa’s savanna-forest transition zone are (ii)______ as well.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　naturalness; anthropogenic
anthropogeniccontiguous
anthropogenic: standard unit headword | contiguous: standard unit headword

Unable to escape their own literary tradition, literary critics either become the ______ of that tradition or, on the contrary, use their knowledge of it to reinterpret writers and trends from new perspectives.Unable to escape their own literary tradition, literary critics either become the ______ of that tradition or, on the contrary, use their knowledge of it to reinterpret writers and trends from new perspectives.

Such a complete (ii)______ of responsibility on the part of the state can hardly be in the public’s interest.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　liberalization; abnegation

答案：A　polemical

Blank 2

#17 TC SINGLE
Studies of hermaphroditic plants may exhibit sampling bias against self-fertilizing and cross-fertilizing species, thus inflating the frequency of species using a mixed mating system (both self-fertilizing and cross-fertilizing); nevertheless, the number of mixed-system species is not ______.Studies of hermaphroditic plants may exhibit sampling bias against self-fertilizing and cross-fertilizing species, thus inflating the frequency of species using a mixed mating system (both self-fertilizing and cross-fertilizing); nevertheless, the number of mixed-system species is not ______.
答案：C　trivial

#18 TC TRIPLE
Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　adapted to; in the water; clumsy for
ancestor
ancestor: standard unit headword

答案：B, D　expel; oust

答案：A　jejune

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　lucrative; authoritative; A staleness

#50 TC TRIPLE
Precipitate interest in Jennifer Francis's theory that anomalously rapid warming of the Arctic disrupts weather patterns in temperate latitudes has (i) ____________ the normal life cycle of a scientific controversy. Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) ____________ before the scientific process has been (iii) ____________.Precipitate interest in Jennifer Francis's theory that anomalously rapid warming of the Arctic disrupts weather patterns in temperate latitudes has (i) ____________ the normal life cycle of a scientific controversy. Usually data are compiled, a hypothesis is tested, and conclusions might then become news if deemed solid. Because Francis's idea links climate change to severe weather, it resonated immediately with the media and the public, and Francis has been forced to (ii) ____________ before the scientific process has been (iii) ____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　inverted; defend her findings; concluded
temperate
temperate: standard unit headword
#51 TC SINGLE
The cognitive flexibility of successful fictional detectives is often ______ by their cultural ambivalence: detectives’ intellectual acumen, it seems, exists in direct proportion to their uneasy place in society.The cognitive flexibility of successful fictional detectives is often ______ by their cultural ambivalence: detectives’ intellectual acumen, it seems, exists in direct proportion to their uneasy place in society.
答案：D　matched
highlight
highlight: standard unit headword

Changes made to ecosystem in order to achieve a goal, such as food production or flood control, often _____ significant unforeseen trade-offs between other important products and service the ecosystems provide.Changes made to ecosystem in order to achieve a goal, such as food production or flood control, often _____ significant unforeseen trade-offs between other important products and service the ecosystems provide.
答案：E, F　yield; engender
engender
engender: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　tepid; retain their ability
dissemble
dissemble: standard unit headword
#54 TC TRIPLE
The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　More than; affirmative; recovery
mandate
mandate: standard unit headword
#55 TC SINGLE
Like her literary heroine, George Eliot, Barbara Kingsolvers is an old-fashioned ______, deeply curious about all branches of human learning.Like her literary heroine, George Eliot, Barbara Kingsolvers is an old-fashioned ______, deeply curious about all branches of human learning.
答案：B　polymath

tyro: standard unit headword
#56 SE
In 1884, Sewall and Dow agreed to join Roosevelt in the Dakota territory for reasons that appear to have been ______: Sewell later recalled that Roosevelt guaranteed them a share of anything made in his cattle business.In 1884, Sewall and Dow agreed to join Roosevelt in the Dakota territory for reasons that appear to have been ______: Sewell later recalled that Roosevelt guaranteed them a share of anything made in his cattle business.
答案：A, C　pecuniary; economic

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　irresistible; systemic

Photography (ii)______ modern arts, but when it is used as a tracing tool in order to (iii)______ the difficulties of achieving correct proportion, the resulting art often feels static and lifeless.The (i)______ quality of much contemporary drawing may be attributable to the use of photography as a drawing shortcut. Photography (ii)______ modern arts, but when it is used as a tracing tool in order to (iii)______ the difficulties of achieving correct proportion, the resulting art often feels static and lifeless.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　inert; wonderfully enriched; circumvent

答案：B　aberrant

答案：A, E　purely advisory; routine

But none of the techniques used would be (ii)______ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics’ derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)______ the company’s work.The experimental theater company’s members know that their performances (i)______ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)______ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics’ derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)______ the company’s work.

Blank 2

答案：A, E, I　made demands on; alien to; misunderstood

Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine

Scientific discovery calls for a difficult balance: intrepid advocacy of new ideas must often be ______ by the results of self-imposed trials.Scientific discovery calls for a difficult balance: intrepid advocacy of new ideas must often be ______ by the results of self-imposed trials.
答案：A, B　tempered; moderated
temper
temper: standard unit headword
#103 TC DOUBLE
To abolish the existence of nation-states is neither feasible nor desirable; but insofar as there are collective interests that transcend national boundaries, the (i)______ of nation-states must be (ii)______ to international institutions.To abolish the existence of nation-states is neither feasible nor desirable; but insofar as there are collective interests that transcend national boundaries, the (i)______ of nation-states must be (ii)______ to international institutions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　sovereignty; subordinated
transcend
transcend: standard unit headword
#104 TC SINGLE
The myth of the small town as a repository of innocence and contentment has frequently been ___________ in literature and film by depictions of rural life as plagued by worldly ambitions.The myth of the small town as a repository of innocence and contentment has frequently been ___________ in literature and film by depictions of rural life as plagued by worldly ambitions.
答案：B　interrogated
myth
myth: standard unit headword
#105 TC TRIPLE
Within the culture as a whole, natural science has been so successful that the word scientific is used in (i)______ manner; it is often assumed that to call something “scientific” is to imply that its reliability has been (ii)______ by research whose results cannot reasonably be (iii)______.Within the culture as a whole, natural science has been so successful that the word scientific is used in (i)______ manner; it is often assumed that to call something “scientific” is to imply that its reliability has been (ii)______ by research whose results cannot reasonably be (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　an honorific; established; disputed

manner: standard unit headword
#106 SE
Joshua Gisemba Bagaka found that the pedagogical results of group projects and other engaged learning activities in Kenyan mathematics classrooms were ______; such activities, then, may not be the best way of improving mathematics education.Joshua Gisemba Bagaka found that the pedagogical results of group projects and other engaged learning activities in Kenyan mathematics classrooms were ______; such activities, then, may not be the best way of improving mathematics education.
答案：C, E　mixed; inconsistent
mixed
mixed: standard unit headword
#107 TC DOUBLE
A hallmark of certain nineteenth-century mystery novels was the reform agenda of their authors, who ostensibly sought to expose economic injustice while depicting the seamy underside of urban life. In reality, however, these claims to a radical political agenda were often (i)______, meant to give lurid thrillers the appearance of (ii)______.A hallmark of certain nineteenth-century mystery novels was the reform agenda of their authors, who ostensibly sought to expose economic injustice while depicting the seamy underside of urban life. In reality, however, these claims to a radical political agenda were often (i)______, meant to give lurid thrillers the appearance of (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　disingenuous; high-minded purpose
hallmark
hallmark: standard unit headword
#108 TC SINGLE
Bridges are often offered as ____________ examples of engineering, given that a bridge's structure is out in the open for all to see.Bridges are often offered as ____________ examples of engineering, given that a bridge's structure is out in the open for all to see.
答案：A　intelligible
open
open: standard unit headword
#109 TC TRIPLE
The modern iron suspension bridge dates from the early 19th century, but it did not have (i)______ debut: many early suspension bridges were damaged, if not outright destroyed, by the wind. There were few (ii)______, however, so the form (iii)______.The modern iron suspension bridge dates from the early 19th century, but it did not have (i)______ debut: many early suspension bridges were damaged, if not outright destroyed, by the wind. There were few (ii)______, however, so the form (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　a propitious; practical alternatives; persisted

propitious: standard unit headword

答案：B, F　canned; formulaic

daunting: standard unit headword
#111 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　imagined; defensible

open: standard unit headword
#112 TC SINGLE

答案：A　tempers

The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)______ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)______—he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)______ force in the development and production of a movie.The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)______ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)______—he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)______ force in the development and production of a movie.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　enduring; profit mongers; creative

myth: standard unit headword

答案：A, B　an order; a stability

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　sketchy; an exhaustive

答案：E　propitious

The public loved souvenirs; the critics (ii)______ what they saw as his betrayal of artistic integrity. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)______ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.Keith Haring’s cartoonish art became even more (i)______ than Andy Warhol’s much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. The public loved souvenirs; the critics (ii)______ what they saw as his betrayal of artistic integrity. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)______ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　commercial; decried; sincere effort

答案：B, C　concede; acknowledge

答案：A　invulnerable

open: standard unit headword
#121 TC TRIPLE
Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)______. Instead of (ii)______ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)______. You, not the author, are the master.Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)______. Instead of (ii)______ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)______. You, not the author, are the master.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　empowering; surrendering to; glean precisely what you want from it
manner

答案：B, D　a momentary; an evanescent

答案：E　meretricious

Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)______ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)______ the legend.With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)______ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)______ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)______ the legend.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　laudatory; a deified; debunk

答案：E, F　tempered; modulated

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：A　verisimilitude

Indeed, these studies indicated that the environmental (iii)______ of producing and recycling paper cups were similar to, if not more than, those related to the production, disposal, and recycling of polystyrene cups.Several studies (i)______ the assumption that paper cups, because they were made of natural products, were more environmentally (ii)______ than cups made of plastic (polystyrene). Indeed, these studies indicated that the environmental (iii)______ of producing and recycling paper cups were similar to, if not more than, those related to the production, disposal, and recycling of polystyrene cups.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　dispelled; friendly; costs

答案：C, F　denounces; lambastes

#131 TC SINGLE
In 2009, the author noted that the phrases "genetic therapy" and "gene therapy" had become ubiquitous and were, for that reason, __________, since the work of genetic researchers had yet to result in any actual therapeutic applications.In 2009, the author noted that the phrases "genetic therapy" and "gene therapy" had become ubiquitous and were, for that reason, __________, since the work of genetic researchers had yet to result in any actual therapeutic applications.
答案：A　misleading

Blank 1
Blank 2

As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii)______ actual practices. In reality, gender-based (iii)______ persists.Traditional Vietnamese culture has long promoted the idea of gender equality. Founding myths (i)______ the equal division of labor in child care for mothers and fathers. As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii)______ actual practices. In reality, gender-based (iii)______ persists.
Blank 1

Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　celebrate; incommensurate with; stratification

答案：E, F　anonymous; obscure

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　reject; entrenched

To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory data

答案：D　marionettes

答案：C, F　burgeoning; expansion

The first clarifying step must be to recognize that “democracy” itself can, in the abstract, (i)______ us as we think of our society and our perception of democracy as citizens. The experience of living in a democracy (ii)______ each generation. A feature of democratic is that something that benefits us in one generation may no longer be a benefit to the next. Thus, experiencing democracy in the twenty-first-century is (iii)______ the political order our ancestors cherished in 1901.How does one evaluate the effects of modern democracy on individuals? The first clarifying step must be to recognize that “democracy” itself can, in the abstract, (i)______ us as we think of our society and our perception of democracy as citizens. The experience of living in a democracy (ii)______ each generation. A feature of democratic is that something that benefits us in one generation may no longer be a benefit to the next. Thus, experiencing democracy in the twenty-first-century is (iii)______ the political order our ancestors cherished in 1901.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　mislead; changes for; radically different from

Blank 2

答案：C, D, H　confound; puncture; facile

Blank 2
答案：B, D　a unique; sustained

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　tempered by; playing a large role in; proximity to

Nevertheless, because scientific knowledge of the dynamics of land ice sheets is (i) __________, there is a possibility that the risk of significant melting of land ice (ii) __________.Scientists generally consider a wholesale melting of the Greenland or West Antarctic ice sheets in the twenty-first century unlikely, even if global warming continues. Nevertheless, because scientific knowledge of the dynamics of land ice sheets is (i) __________, there is a possibility that the risk of significant melting of land ice (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　rudimentary; has been underestimated

The bright yellow, mouth-watering treat does not grow in the wild anywhere on the planet, so its (i) is not at all (ii) . GEARS: SkREIIGRE)Despite the abundance and importance of maize, its biological origin has been a long-running mystery. The bright yellow, mouth-watering treat does not grow in the wild anywhere on the planet, so its (i) is not at all (ii) . GEARS: SkREIIGRE)

答案：E　plastic

#154 TC TRIPLE
Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)______ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)______ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)______ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)______ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　attend disproportionately to; responsiveness to; mask
formulate
formulate: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　despair of; faithful

答案：A　superficial

On the one hand, high self-esteem is believed to confer a wide variety of adaptive and affective benefits; on the other, positive self-views have been linked to a number of intrapersonal and interpersonal strategies that can reasonably be described as (ii) __________.High self-esteem has recently assumed a somewhat (i) __________ status in social psychology. On the one hand, high self-esteem is believed to confer a wide variety of adaptive and affective benefits; on the other, positive self-views have been linked to a number of intrapersonal and interpersonal strategies that can reasonably be described as (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　paradoxical; antisocial
elevated
elevated: standard unit headword

#207 TC TRIPLE
Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i) life in the sea. One Maiacetus innus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii) : its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii) walking. [fMSARS: 5kREIIGRE]Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i) life in the sea. One Maiacetus innus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii) : its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii) walking. [fMSARS: 5kREIIGRE]
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　adapted to; in the water; clumsy for

ancestor: standard unit headword

Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the single purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii) —he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii) force in the development and production of a movie.The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i) one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. The Chippewa regarded their treaties with the federal government as solemn covenants and therefore, throughout the twentieth century, met efforts by governmental officials to __________ those treaties with both open and hidden resistance.The Chippewa regarded their treaties with the federal government as solemn covenants and therefore, throughout the twentieth century, met efforts by governmental officials to __________ those treaties with both open and hidden resistance.
答案：B, E　abrogate; nullify

open: standard unit headword

答案：C, D　assumed; counted on

However, the (i) of this (ii) is not (iii) : if pushed too far, the biosphere might become a net source of COz, thereby accelerating climate change and causing a positive feedback loop.It appears that the terrestrial biosphere has been absorbing a significant fraction of anthropogenic COz, thereby slowing down the rate of global climate change. However, the (i) of this (ii) is not (iii) : if pushed too far, the biosphere might become a net source of COz, thereby accelerating climate change and causing a positive feedback loop.

答案：D, F　brazen; barefaced

Melodrama is widely perceived as antithetical to cinematic realism and thus, from a perspective that seeks (ii) __________ rendering of the historical past, incapable of delivering the necessary (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　pejorative; a seemingly unmediated; objectivity
pejorative
pejorative: standard unit headword

Hilton discusses the well-developed capacity among the most influential and talented Victorians for writing on a wide range of topics, thus highlighting their lordly __________ specialization.Hilton discusses the well-developed capacity among the most influential and talented Victorians for writing on a wide range of topics, thus highlighting their lordly __________ specialization.
答案：E, F　disdain for; scorn for

#258 TC TRIPLE
An innovative musician, accompanist extraordinaire, and protomodern hipster, Lester Young (1909-1959) remains a (i) in the jazz firmament. Some of the reasons are obvious: although he dressed with conspicuous cool, he chose an artistic trajectory that seemed calculated to (ii) the spotlight-his best work was in the bands of other musicians, such as Billie Holiday and Count Basie, rather than at the front of his own. Moreover, he was shy, subtle, and sensitive- traits not usually associated with great (ii) .An innovative musician, accompanist extraordinaire, and protomodern hipster, Lester Young (1909-1959) remains a (i) in the jazz firmament. Some of the reasons are obvious: although he dressed with conspicuous cool, he chose an artistic trajectory that seemed calculated to (ii) the spotlight-his best work was in the bands of other musicians, such as Billie Holiday and Count Basie, rather than at the front of his own. Moreover, he was shy, subtle, and sensitive- traits not usually associated with great (ii) .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　lesser light; shun; celebrity
calculated

答案：B, F　ubiquitous; widespread

It is crucial, however, to (ii) . In other words, one needs to resist (iii) generic “manners” with more linguistically and historically precise designations, no least because Austen herself distinguishes.One of the problems with recent books that perpetuate the view of Jane Austen as moral tutor, a sort of Miss Manners for the ages, is that they understand manners as (i) , aS near-universal and timeless behavioral ideals or, worse still, a set of rules to be followed. Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　monolithic; differentiate various; conflating

#266 TC TRIPLE
The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　cumulative; heretical departures; jarring
jarring
jarring: standard unit headword

答案：B, D　sybaritic; hedonistic

The computer modelers who are their potential collaborators can (iii) __________ by taking a dismissive attitude toward scientists who have relatively little quantitative training.

#270 TC TRIPLE
Seigel has written an important and invaluable book on the history of Western ideas of the self, showing a genuine (i) __________ in analyzing philosophical views of often daunting intricacy. Though a historian by profession, he keeps to a minimum his excursions into the (ii) __________ of ideas, recognizing that great minds often (iii) __________ the preconceptions of their age.Seigel has written an important and invaluable book on the history of Western ideas of the self, showing a genuine (i) __________ in analyzing philosophical views of often daunting intricacy. Though a historian by profession, he keeps to a minimum his excursions into the (ii) __________ of ideas, recognizing that great minds often (iii) __________ the preconceptions of their age.

Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　acuity; social and cultural context; outstrip

#271 SE

答案：B, D　strident; shrill

答案：C, F　engender; construct

#273 TC TRIPLE
The guarantee of academic freedom to professors is intended to encourage intellectual independence. But so long as faculty members receive training in a specific academic discipline and are appointed to positions defined by discipline, and so long as colleges and universities are organized by departments that (i) __________ long-standing disciplinary traditions, academic freedom will also (ii) __________ unique discipline-specific expectations. Yet the right to (iii) __________ disciplinary paradigms is also fundamental to academic freedom.The guarantee of academic freedom to professors is intended to encourage intellectual independence. But so long as faculty members receive training in a specific academic discipline and are appointed to positions defined by discipline, and so long as colleges and universities are organized by departments that (i) __________ long-standing disciplinary traditions, academic freedom will also (ii) __________ unique discipline-specific expectations. Yet the right to (iii) __________ disciplinary paradigms is also fundamental to academic freedom.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　inherit; tend to embody; resist
transcendflout
transcend: standard unit headword | flout: standard unit headword

答案：D, F　sluggish; dilatory

Consequently, these treatments are widely used.For almost two centuries, the German island of Sylt has offered various therapies for every conceivable (i)______, from broken bones to liver complaints. The local mud, saltwater, thermal pools, and spas have been deemed (ii)______ by the German medical system, which (iii)______ some of these treatments. Consequently, these treatments are widely used.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　malady; healthful; funds

#276 TC TRIPLE
Neolithic people living in an arid northern region of China cultivated common millet more than did people living to their south. Common millet is easy to cultivate and appears to have been better suited to primitive agricultural practices than were other crops. Under (i) __________ environmental conditions, however, common millet cultivation may be (ii) __________ because of the millet's relatively (iii) __________ response to high soil fertility and abundant water availability.Neolithic people living in an arid northern region of China cultivated common millet more than did people living to their south. Common millet is easy to cultivate and appears to have been better suited to primitive agricultural practices than were other crops. Under (i) __________ environmental conditions, however, common millet cultivation may be (ii) __________ because of the millet's relatively (iii) __________ response to high soil fertility and abundant water availability.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　propitious; a disadvantageous choice; limited

答案：B, F　familiar; recognizable

答案：C, F　trivial; inconsequential

In recent years, though, it has become clear that, for all its excellence, public radio will continue to lose viewers to its flashy but superficial rivals.

答案：A, B　diminutive; minuscular

Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)______ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)______, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)______ of autobiography: the procession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)______ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)______, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy
antithesis

But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　a panacea; materialize; encourage

#347 TC TRIPLE
Although often criticized by former allies for having abandoned or betrayed their common cause, Nguyen was actually remarkably (i) __________ over the years. Examination of her career shows that she never ceased to use the best data she could find to illuminate the issues. If the data undermined positions she had previously (ii) __________, she followed the data. If the data confirmed her earlier views, she clearly stated the fact. If the data were (iii) __________, she counseled caution.Although often criticized by former allies for having abandoned or betrayed their common cause, Nguyen was actually remarkably (i) __________ over the years. Examination of her career shows that she never ceased to use the best data she could find to illuminate the issues. If the data undermined positions she had previously (ii) __________, she followed the data. If the data confirmed her earlier views, she clearly stated the fact. If the data were (iii) __________, she counseled caution.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　consistent; espoused; insufficient

答案：B, E　irresistible; systemic

#355 TC TRIPLE
Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　monolithic; supposed distinction; deleterious

[#M{ZARS: sk#/NMGRE)

#357 TC TRIPLE
Because the term "memoir," unlike the term "autobiography," can suggest subjectivity rather than objectivity, Barton, in choosing to call his book a memoir, (i) __________ the accusation that his story may be (ii) __________. As Barton himself has noted, memoir promises only to give the reader an account of the author's memory, and no one but the author can therefore (iii) __________.Because the term "memoir," unlike the term "autobiography," can suggest subjectivity rather than objectivity, Barton, in choosing to call his book a memoir, (i) __________ the accusation that his story may be (ii) __________. As Barton himself has noted, memoir promises only to give the reader an account of the author's memory, and no one but the author can therefore (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　deflects; untrue; gauge its veracity

In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Since verisimilitude is sought, the aim in adding apparent age to the new surface must be to (i) __________, circumventing the human eye's adeptness at spotting anything that seems (ii) __________. Different framers have different ways of (iii) __________ the effect of chance.In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Since verisimilitude is sought, the aim in adding apparent age to the new surface must be to (i) __________, circumventing the human eye's adeptness at spotting anything that seems (ii) __________. Different framers have different ways of (iii) __________ the effect of chance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　conceal the artifice; intentional; mimicking

The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii) by multiple epidemiological studies. 答案：A, C　exigent; imperative
